Stuart Russell.topics: rational agent, probabilistic reasoning, reinforcement learning, value consistency, AI safety
Stuart Russell.key_contributions: Author of the standard textbook ``Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach'' (co-authored with Norvig). Leading the systemization of AI based on the rational agent view and research on AI safety and value consistency.
Stuart Russell.inclusion_reason: Author of the standard textbook on modern AI and winner of the IJCAI Research Excellence Award. The impact on the entire field is extremely large.

Judea Pearl.topics: Bayesian network, causal inference, do operator, probabilistic graphical model
Judea Pearl.key_contributions: Established the mathematical foundations of Bayesian networks and causal inference. It revolutionized AI through probabilistic and causal reasoning.
Judea Pearl.inclusion_reason: Turing Award winner and founder of probability and causal inference. The contribution to the basic theory of AI is decisive.

Geoffrey Hinton.topics: Backpropagation, Boltzmann machine, deep learning, representation learning
Geoffrey Hinton.key_contributions: Popularization of error backpropagation method and establishment of basic theories of neural networks such as Boltzmann machines and deep belief networks. A central figure in the deep learning revolution.
Geoffrey Hinton.inclusion_reason: A master of the basic theory of neural networks, he won the Turing Prize and the Nobel Prize. He also won the IJCAI Research Excellence Award.

Rina Dechter.topics: Constraint satisfaction problem, probabilistic inference, search algorithm, bucket elimination, tree width
Rina Dechter.key_contributions: Fundamental contributions to unified algorithmic frameworks for constraint satisfaction and probabilistic reasoning (bucket elimination, etc.), modeling, complexity analysis, and unification principles.
Rina Dechter.inclusion_reason: Recipient of the 2025 IJCAI Research Excellence Award as a researcher who built the foundations of constraint satisfaction and probabilistic reasoning.

Eugene Freuder.topics: Constraint-based inference, constraint satisfaction problem, consistency algorithm
Eugene Freuder.key_contributions: Pioneering research on constraint-based reasoning and problem solving. Builds a foundation for consistency/search for constraint satisfaction problems.
Eugene Freuder.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in the field of constraint satisfaction and winner of the 2020 IJCAI Research Excellence Award.

Bart Selman.topics: Satisfiability problem, local search (GSAT/WalkSAT), phase transition phenomenon, knowledge compilation
Bart Selman.key_contributions: Solving SAT using stochastic local searches such as GSAT/WalkSAT, and discovering phase transition phenomena in calculation problems. Former president of AAAI.
Bart Selman.inclusion_reason: A master of SAT solution and exploration, winner of the first IJCAI John McCarthy Award, and former president of AAAI.

Hector Geffner.topics: Domain-independent heuristic planning, model-based AI, breadth-based search, generalization planning
Hector Geffner.key_contributions: Establishment of domain-independent heuristic planning, breadth-based search, and integrated research on model-based and database AI.
Hector Geffner.inclusion_reason: A leading authority on automated planning and an AAAI Fellow. laid the foundation for heuristic planning.

Malte Helmert.topics: heuristic search, Fast Downward planner, planning complexity, abstract heuristic
Malte Helmert.key_contributions: Development of a Fast Downward planning system, the theoretical basis of complexity analysis and abstraction-based heuristics for planning problems.
Malte Helmert.inclusion_reason: The center of active planning research. Fast Downward is a standard planner in the field and has a great influence.

Subbarao Kambhampati.topics: Automated Planning, Human Recognition Planning, Explainable AI Planning, LLM and Planning
Subbarao Kambhampati.key_contributions: Research on automatic planning and human-aware planning, explainability of planning. Leading the field as past president of AAAI.
Subbarao Kambhampati.inclusion_reason: Planning master and former AAAI president. Born in India, he also contributes to the diversity of Asia.

Sven Koenig.topics: Heuristic search, multi-agent pathfinding (MAPF), robot decision making, D* Lite
Sven Koenig.key_contributions: Basic research on planning and collaboration, heuristic search, and multi-agent path searching for robots and agents.
Sven Koenig.inclusion_reason: AAAI Fellow and core researcher in search, planning, and multi-agent route searching.

Toby Walsh.topics: Constraint programming, satisfiability, fair distribution, voting theory, social impact of AI
Toby Walsh.key_contributions: Research on constraint programming and satisfiability, computational social selection. He is also famous for raising awareness about the social impact of AI.
Toby Walsh.inclusion_reason: Constraint satisfaction/SAT master and AAAI Fellow. Based in Australia, we also contribute to regional diversity.

Joao Marques-Silva.topics: Competition-driven clause learning (CDCL), satisfiability solving, formally explainable AI, MaxSAT
Joao Marques-Silva.key_contributions: The invention of competition-driven clause learning (CDCL) dramatically improved the performance of modern SAT solvers. In recent years, he has been researching formally explainable AI.
Joao Marques-Silva.inclusion_reason: He is the inventor of CDCL and laid the foundation for modern SAT solving technology.

Armin Biere.topics: SAT solver (Lingeling/Kissat), bounded model checking, hardware verification
Armin Biere.key_contributions: Developed high performance SAT solvers (PicoSAT, Lingeling, Kissat) and won many SAT competitions. A pioneer of bounded model checking.
Armin Biere.inclusion_reason: A leading empirical researcher who developed the world's highest performance SAT solver group.

Hector Levesque.topics: Knowledge representation, epistemic logic, situational calculation, boundaries of accessibility, Winograd Schema
Hector Levesque.key_contributions: Theory of knowledge representation and inference, computability boundaries of representation and inference, situational computation and epistemic logic. Proposed Winograd Schema Challenge.
Hector Levesque.inclusion_reason: A master of knowledge representation and reasoning, winner of the 2013 IJCAI Research Excellence Award.

Raymond Reiter.topics: Default logic, non-monotonic reasoning, frame problems, situational calculation, cognitive robotics
Raymond Reiter.key_contributions: He created default logic and laid the foundation for non-monotonic reasoning. Contributed to the development of situational computation and cognitive robotics (GOLOG).
Raymond Reiter.inclusion_reason: Founder of non-monotonic reasoning and default logic and winner of the 1993 IJCAI Research Excellence Award. A classic master.

Robert Kowalski.topics: Logic programming, Prolog theory, abduction, event calculation
Robert Kowalski.key_contributions: Established the theoretical basis (procedural interpretation) of logical programming and supported the theory of Prolog. Joint proposal for event calculation.
Robert Kowalski.inclusion_reason: Father of logic programming and winner of the 2011 IJCAI Research Excellence Award. A classic master.

Vladimir Lifschitz.topics: Answer set programming (ASP), stable model semantics, nonmonotonic reasoning, action theory
Vladimir Lifschitz.key_contributions: Introduced stable model semantics with Gelfond and established the term and framework of answer set programming (ASP).
Vladimir Lifschitz.inclusion_reason: Founder of answer set programming and a central figure in the field of logic programming.

Michael Gelfond.topics: Answer set programming (ASP), stable model semantics, declarative problem solving
Michael Gelfond.key_contributions: Define stable model (answer set) semantics for logical programs with Lifschitz. One of the founders of ASP.
Michael Gelfond.inclusion_reason: Co-proponent of stable model semantics and founder of ASP. Laid the foundations of logical programming.

Torsten Schaub.topics: Answer set programming, clingo/clasp solver, non-monotonic inference
Torsten Schaub.key_contributions: Developed the high-performance ASP system Potassco (clingo, clasp) and put ASP into practical use. Leading the ASP competition.
Torsten Schaub.inclusion_reason: EurAI/AAAI Fellow and a leading developer of a practical solver for ASP.

Franz Baader.topics: Descriptive logic, ontological reasoning, term rewriting, unified theory
Franz Baader.key_contributions: Established the theoretical foundations of Description Logic and compiled the ``Description Logic Handbook''. The core of ontological reasoning.
Franz Baader.inclusion_reason: A central figure who built the theoretical foundations of descriptive logic. Contributing to the foundation of the Semantic Web.

Ian Horrocks.topics: Descriptive logical reasoning, OWL ontology language, tableau reasoning, ontology engineering
Ian Horrocks.key_contributions: Highly efficient inference techniques for description logic and design of OWL ontology language. Building the foundation of the Semantic Web.
Ian Horrocks.inclusion_reason: A central figure in OWL and descriptive logical reasoning and a Fellow of the Royal Society.

Deborah McGuinness.topics: Semantic web, ontology, descriptive logic, provenance
Deborah McGuinness.key_contributions: Significant contributions to the Semantic Web, knowledge representation and reasoning environments, and practical AI applications. Participated in OWL design.
Deborah McGuinness.inclusion_reason: A central figure in Semantic Web and ontology research and an AAAI Fellow. It also contributes to the diversity of female researchers.

Lise Getoor.topics: Statistical relational learning, probabilistic soft logic (PSL), graph mining, knowledge discovery
Lise Getoor.key_contributions: Establishment of a method (Statistical Relationship Learning, PSL) that integrates probabilistic and logical representations. Graph mining and network analysis.
Lise Getoor.inclusion_reason: AAAI Fellow and a leading expert on statistical relationship learning that integrates probabilistic reasoning and logic. It also contributes to the diversity of female researchers.

Daphne Koller.topics: Probabilistic graphical model, Bayesian network, relational probabilistic model, computational biology
Daphne Koller.key_contributions: Theory and applications of probabilistic graphical models, standard textbook ``Probabilistic Graphical Models'' (co-authored with Friedman).
Daphne Koller.inclusion_reason: He is a master of probabilistic graphical models and has won the IJCAI Computers and Thought Award and the ACM Prize.

Michael I. Jordan.topics: Variational inference, graphical models, nonparametric Bayes, optimization and learning
Michael I. Jordan.key_contributions: Established the theoretical foundations of variational inference, approximate inference for graphical models, and statistical machine learning.
Michael I. Jordan.inclusion_reason: A master of probabilistic inference and machine learning, winner of the 2016 IJCAI Research Excellence Award.

Yoav Shoham.topics: Multi-agent systems, algorithmic game theory, agent-oriented programming, temporal logic
Yoav Shoham.key_contributions: Proposal of multi-agent systems, algorithmic game theory, and agent-oriented programming.
Yoav Shoham.inclusion_reason: A master of multi-agent systems and winner of the 2019 IJCAI Research Excellence Award.

Moshe Tennenholtz.topics: Algorithmic game theory, social selection, recommendation systems, artificial social systems
Moshe Tennenholtz.key_contributions: Research on the boundary between artificial social systems, algorithmic game theory, and AI. Theory of cooperation and non-cooperation.
Moshe Tennenholtz.inclusion_reason: He is a leading researcher in the boundary area between AI and game theory and won the 2016 IJCAI John McCarthy Award.

Dan Roth.topics: Learning-based reasoning, constrained conditional models, common sense reasoning, natural language understanding
Dan Roth.key_contributions: Basic research on integrating learning and inference (constrained conditional models) and applying machine learning to natural language understanding.
Dan Roth.inclusion_reason: A leading researcher on the integration of learning and inference and winner of the 2017 IJCAI John McCarthy Award.

Barbara Grosz.topics: Discourse structure, cooperative planning, human-agent cooperation, dialogue theory
Barbara Grosz.key_contributions: Laid the foundation for discourse structure theory and collaborative multi-agent planning (SharedPlans).
Barbara Grosz.inclusion_reason: A master of discourse theory and collaborative planning, winner of the 2015 IJCAI Research Excellence Award, and former president of AAAI. It also contributes to the diversity of female researchers.

Alan Bundy.topics: Automatic theorem proving, inductive proof and rippling, ontology evolution, automatic construction of knowledge representation
Alan Bundy.key_contributions: Proposed automatic theorem proving and proof planning (rippling) for mathematical reasoning.
Alan Bundy.inclusion_reason: He is a master of automatic reasoning and theorem proving, won the 2007 IJCAI Research Excellence Award, and is a Fellow of the Royal Society.

Victor Lesser.topics: Distributed artificial intelligence, multi-agent system, blackboard architecture, collaborative problem solving
Victor Lesser.key_contributions: One of the founders of distributed artificial intelligence and multi-agent systems. Fundamentals of blackboard systems and collaborative problem solving.
Victor Lesser.inclusion_reason: Founder of distributed AI/multi-agent and winner of the 2009 IJCAI Research Excellence Award.

Nils Nilsson.topics: A*Exploration, STRIPS planner, Shakey robot, logic-based AI
Nils Nilsson.key_contributions: Development of A* search algorithm, STRIPS planning, and Shakey robot. A classic master who wrote the basic AI textbook.
Nils Nilsson.inclusion_reason: Developer of A* and STRIPS and winner of the 2003 IJCAI Research Excellence Award. A classic AI master.

Edward Feigenbaum.topics: Expert systems, knowledge engineering, DENDRAL, MYCIN
Edward Feigenbaum.key_contributions: Father of expert systems (DENDRAL, MYCIN) and knowledge engineering. Laid the foundation for knowledge-based AI.
Edward Feigenbaum.inclusion_reason: Father of knowledge engineering and winner of the Turing Award. A classic AI master and former president of AAAI.

Leslie Pack Kaelbling.topics: Planning under uncertainty (POMDP), reinforcement learning, task & motion planning, robot decision making
Leslie Pack Kaelbling.key_contributions: Planning under uncertainty such as POMDP, integration of reinforcement learning and robotics. Founding editor-in-chief of JMLR.
Leslie Pack Kaelbling.inclusion_reason: He is a master of planning under uncertainty and reinforcement learning and won the IJCAI Computers and Thought Award. It also contributes to the diversity of female researchers.

Manuela Veloso.topics: Case-based planning, RoboCup soccer, autonomous agent, planning learning
Manuela Veloso.key_contributions: Co-founder of RoboCup, case-based planning and planning learning. Leading autonomous robot agent research. Former president of AAAI.
Manuela Veloso.inclusion_reason: Master of planning and multi-agent robotics, past president of AAAI, and recipient of the IJCAI John McCarthy Award.

Francesca Rossi.topics: Constraint programming, soft constraints, preference inference, AI ethics and value alignment
Francesca Rossi.key_contributions: A leader in constraint programming, soft constraint/preference theory, and AI ethics research. Former president of IJCAI and former president of AAAI.
Francesca Rossi.inclusion_reason: Expert in constraint programming and AI ethics and former president of IJCAI/AAAI. Contributing to the diversity of female researchers and industry.

Zhi-Hua Zhou.topics: Ensemble learning, weakly supervised learning, multi-label learning, deep forest
Zhi-Hua Zhou.key_contributions: Theory of ensemble learning/weakly supervised learning, deep forest (gcForest). Founded LAMDA Group and led AI in China. AAAI 2019 Program Committee Chair.
Zhi-Hua Zhou.inclusion_reason: China's leading AI researcher and master of ensemble/weakly supervised learning. Fellow of multiple societies.

Qiang Yang.topics: Transfer learning, federated learning, plan recognition, data mining
Qiang Yang.key_contributions: He laid the foundation for transfer learning and federated learning, and also researched early planning such as plan recognition. Served as IJCAI Chairman (2017-19).
Qiang Yang.inclusion_reason: A world authority on transfer and federated learning and former president of IJCAI. Based in Hong Kong and China, we also contribute to diversity.

Shun-ichi Amari.topics: Information geometry, natural gradient method, learning theory, stochastic neuron
Shun-ichi Amari.key_contributions: Established the mathematical foundations of neural networks and created information geometry and natural gradient methods. A pioneer in learning theory.
Shun-ichi Amari.inclusion_reason: A world-renowned master of the mathematical foundations of neural networks and the founder of information geometry. Japan's leading NN researcher.

Kunihiko Fukushima.topics: Neocognitron, convolutional neural network, visual cortex model, self-organization
Kunihiko Fukushima.key_contributions: In 1979, he developed the first multilayer convolutional neural network ``Neocognitron'', creating the prototype for modern deep CNNs.
Kunihiko Fukushima.inclusion_reason: Inventor of the neocognitron, the prototype of convolutional neural networks. A master who created the origins of deep learning.

John Hopfield.topics: Hopfield network, associative memory, energy model, collective computation
John Hopfield.key_contributions: He proposed an associative memory model using Hopfield networks and laid the physical foundations for neural networks.
John Hopfield.inclusion_reason: A proponent of the Hopfield net and a master of the basic theory of neural networks. Winner of the 2024 Nobel Prize in Physics.

Teuvo Kohonen.topics: Self-organizing map (SOM), associative memory, learning vector quantization, unsupervised learning
Teuvo Kohonen.key_contributions: Invented self-organizing maps (SOM) and laid the foundation for unsupervised neural learning. A pioneer in associative memory research.
Teuvo Kohonen.inclusion_reason: Inventor of self-organizing maps and master of basic neural network theory. Center of European NN research.

Jürgen Schmidhuber.topics: LSTM, recurrent neural network, meta-learning, artificial general intelligence theory
Jürgen Schmidhuber.key_contributions: Jointly developed LSTM (long short-term memory) and laid the foundation for recurrent neural networks. Also researches meta-learning and general AI theory.
Jürgen Schmidhuber.inclusion_reason: Co-developer of LSTM and a central figure in the basic theory of neural networks. A leading figure in European NN research such as ICANN.

Yoshua Bengio.topics: Representation learning, attention mechanism, generative model, deep learning theory, AI safety
Yoshua Bengio.key_contributions: Contributed to the theoretical foundations of deep learning and the development of representation learning, neural language models, and attention mechanisms. A central figure in the deep learning revolution.
Yoshua Bengio.inclusion_reason: A master of neural network basic theory and deep learning, winner of the Turing Award.

Christopher Bishop.topics: Neural network pattern recognition, probabilistic machine learning, Bayesian inference, mixed density networks
Christopher Bishop.key_contributions: Author of the standard textbooks ``Neural Networks for Pattern Recognition'' and ``Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning.'' Systematizing probabilistic machine learning.
Christopher Bishop.inclusion_reason: Author of standard textbooks on neural networks and machine learning and Fellow of the Royal Society. A leading figure in industrial research.

Stephen Grossberg.topics: Adaptive resonance theory (ART), competitive learning, cognitive neurodynamics, visual memory model
Stephen Grossberg.key_contributions: He proposed the adaptive resonance theory (ART) and laid the theoretical basis for self-organization and competitive learning. Launched Neural Networks magazine and founder of INNS.
Stephen Grossberg.inclusion_reason: A proponent of adaptive resonance theory and a master of basic neural network theory. Founder of INNS.

Byoung-Tak Zhang.topics: Molecular computing/DNA computing learning, Bayesian evolutionary computing, cognitive robotics, developmental learning
Byoung-Tak Zhang.key_contributions: Research on learning models based on biomolecular computation such as evolutionary learning, neural computation, and hypernetworks. Leading Korean AI research.
Byoung-Tak Zhang.inclusion_reason: Korea's leading neural/machine learning researcher. Contributed to ICONIP, etc. and contributed to the diversity of Asia.

Nicholas R. Jennings.topics: Multi-agent systems, autonomous agents, negotiation between agents, algorithmic mechanism design
Nicholas R. Jennings.key_contributions: Fundamental research on the theory of multi-agent systems and autonomous agents, negotiation among agents, and mechanism design.
Nicholas R. Jennings.inclusion_reason: A master of multi-agent systems and winner of the IJCAI Computers and Thought Award.

Carla Gomes.topics: Constraint inference, randomization and restart of exploration, computational sustainability, heavy tail phenomenon
Carla Gomes.key_contributions: Discovered the heavy tail phenomenon of combinatorial search and the randomized restart strategy. Founded the field of Computational Sustainability.
Carla Gomes.inclusion_reason: Founder of Constraint Reasoning/Exploration and Computational Sustainability and AAAI Fellow. It also contributes to the diversity of female researchers.

Adnan Darwiche.topics: Bayesian network inference, knowledge compilation, probabilistic circuits, formal foundations of explainable AI
Adnan Darwiche.key_contributions: Theoretical foundations of probabilistic inference using knowledge compilation and arithmetic circuits, and exact inference of Bayesian networks. Formal explainable AI.
Adnan Darwiche.inclusion_reason: Master of probabilistic reasoning and knowledge compilation and AAAI/ACM Fellow. Being from Lebanon also contributes to diversity.

Katsumi Inoue.topics: Abduction reasoning, logic programming, inductive logic programming, logic applications to systems biology
Katsumi Inoue.key_contributions: Theory of hypothetical inference (abduction) and logical programming, and applications to logic-based learning and systems biology.
Katsumi Inoue.inclusion_reason: A central figure in Japanese logical programming and knowledge representation research. Contributing to the diversity of Asia and symbolic AI.

Hiroaki Kitano.topics: RoboCup, systems biology, evolutionary computation, scientific discovery by AI
Hiroaki Kitano.key_contributions: Founder of RoboCup, establishment of systems biology, and advocate of scientific discovery using AI (Novel Turing Challenge).
Hiroaki Kitano.inclusion_reason: Founder of RoboCup and winner of the IJCAI Computers and Thought Award. Japan's leading AI researcher.

Miroslaw Truszczynski.topics: Nonmonotonic reasoning, answer set programming, default logic, declarative problem solving
Miroslaw Truszczynski.key_contributions: An important contribution to the theory of nonmonotonic reasoning and answer set programming. Mathematical basis of default logic and ASP.
Miroslaw Truszczynski.inclusion_reason: AAAI Fellow and core researcher on non-monotonic reasoning and ASP theory. Being from Poland also contributes to diversity.

Aravind Joshi.topics: Tree-joining grammar (TAG), computational linguistics, discourse, formal grammar theory
Aravind Joshi.key_contributions: He proposed tree joining grammar (TAG) and laid the foundations for computational linguistics and formal grammar theory.
Aravind Joshi.inclusion_reason: A master of computational linguistics and formal grammar, he won the 1997 IJCAI Research Excellence Award. Being from India also contributes to diversity.

Jitendra Malik.topics: Image segmentation, object recognition/detection, human pose estimation, robotics perception
Jitendra Malik.key_contributions: Built many foundations for modern CV such as Normalized Cuts, contour/texture description, and R-CNN series object detection. Leading image segmentation and object category recognition.
Jitendra Malik.inclusion_reason: One of the most influential researchers in modern computer vision. Winner of numerous test-of-time awards and PAMI Distinguished Researcher awards.

Andrew Zisserman.topics: Multi-view geometry, visual recognition, large-scale image/video retrieval, deep CNN (VGG)
Andrew Zisserman.key_contributions: Standard textbook of multi-view geometry (Hartley & Zisserman), VGGNet, large-scale video search. Founder of VGG Group.
Andrew Zisserman.inclusion_reason: World authority on multi-view geometry and visual recognition. Royal Society Fellow, 3 Marr Prize winners.

Kaiming He.topics: Residual network (ResNet), object detection/segmentation (Mask R-CNN), self-supervised learning (MAE)
Kaiming He.key_contributions: ResNet (residual learning) enables deep NN learning and has produced many of the most highly cited studies such as Faster/Mask R-CNN and MAE.
Kaiming He.metrics: approx. 800,000+; ResNet is among the most-cited papers
Kaiming He.inclusion_reason: Lead author of ResNet/Mask R-CNN/MAE. One of the researchers who had the most influence on deep learning and CV as a whole.

Jian Sun.topics: Residual network (ResNet), efficient CNN (ShuffleNet), image processing
Jian Sun.key_contributions: Co-author of ResNet, ShuffleNet, MegEngine. Leads image processing and CV research at MSRA. Passed away in June 2022.
Jian Sun.inclusion_reason: Major co-author of ResNet, symbolic leader of the Chinese CV world. Established Megvii Research.

Fei-Fei Li.topics: Large-scale image recognition (ImageNet), scene understanding, vision and language
Fei-Fei Li.key_contributions: Built ImageNet and ILSVRC and inspired the deep learning revolution. A leader in scene understanding and visual language research.
Fei-Fei Li.inclusion_reason: We pioneered the era of deep learning through ImageNet. One of the most prominent researchers in the CV field.

Takeo Kanade.topics: Optical flow (Lucas-Kanade), face/shape recognition, shape-from-X, robotics
Takeo Kanade.key_contributions: Established the basics of CV such as the Lucas-Kanade method, KLT tracker, shape-from-interreflections, etc. Leading the CMU Robotics Institute.
Takeo Kanade.inclusion_reason: A pioneering master of computer vision. Received the Kyoto Prize. Originally from Japan, he is a world authority in the CV world.

Katsushi Ikeuchi.topics: Physics-based vision, shape-from-X, e-Heritage (digitization of cultural assets), robot learning
Katsushi Ikeuchi.key_contributions: Physics-based vision, shape estimation from mutual reflection, 3D digitization of cultural assets, observational learning of robots.
Katsushi Ikeuchi.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in physically-based visual perception and digitization of cultural assets. Awarded PAMI Distinguished Researcher and is a major figure in the Japanese CV world.

Rama Chellappa.topics: Face recognition, person/action recognition from video, statistical image model
Rama Chellappa.key_contributions: Many years of contributions to face and gait recognition, video analysis, and statistical image modeling.
Rama Chellappa.inclusion_reason: A long-time leading researcher in face recognition and video analysis. Received the PAMI Distinguished Researcher Award.

Pietro Perona.topics: Object/category recognition, visual attention, datasets (Caltech, COCO), computational behavior
Pietro Perona.key_contributions: Perona-Malik Anisotropic Diffusion, Visual Category Recognition, Caltech/COCO Dataset.
Pietro Perona.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in visual recognition and dataset construction. Received the PAMI Distinguished Researcher Award.

William T. Freeman.topics: Computational photography, image prior distribution, Motion Magnification, Bayesian vision
William T. Freeman.key_contributions: Research on steerable filters, super resolution, motion magnification, and image statistics.
William T. Freeman.inclusion_reason: An authority on computational photography and Bayesian vision. Received the PAMI Distinguished Researcher Award.

Trevor Darrell.topics: Object detection (R-CNN), domain adaptation, visual language, representation learning
Trevor Darrell.key_contributions: R-CNN, Caffe (joint), domain adaptation/visual language research.
Trevor Darrell.inclusion_reason: Leading researcher in object detection and deep representation learning. Longuet-Higgins Award on R-CNN.

Ross Girshick.topics: Object detection (R-CNN, Fast/Faster R-CNN), segmentation (Mask R-CNN), YOLO, SAM
Ross Girshick.key_contributions: Fundamentals of object detection and segmentation such as R-CNN series, YOLO, Mask R-CNN, and Segment Anything (SAM).
Ross Girshick.inclusion_reason: Leading developer of modern object detection. Involved in R-CNN/YOLO/Mask R-CNN/SAM.

Ali Farhadi.topics: Object Detection (YOLO), Visual Reasoning, Efficient Models, Open Source LLM
Ali Farhadi.key_contributions: YOLO object detection, XNOR-Net, visual attributes and inference. Leads AI2 and promotes an open model.
Ali Farhadi.inclusion_reason: Co-developer of YOLO and CEO of AI2. An influential researcher who straddles CV and AI.

Cordelia Schmid.topics: Image search, object recognition, video understanding/action recognition, visual language
Cordelia Schmid.key_contributions: Numerous basic methods for local feature/image search, action recognition, and video understanding.
Cordelia Schmid.inclusion_reason: Europe's leading CV expert. Winner of numerous test-of-time awards and Körber awards for image search and video understanding.

Andrew Davison.topics: Visual SLAM, Real-time 3D reconstruction, Spatial AI
Andrew Davison.key_contributions: Established real-time visual SLAM/3D reconstruction such as MonoSLAM, DTAM, KinectFusion, etc. Advocates Spatial AI.
Andrew Davison.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in visual SLAM and real-time 3D reconstruction. Co-founded SLAMcore.

Richard Newcombe.topics: Real-time high-density 3D reconstruction (KinectFusion, DTAM), SLAM, Egocentric perception
Richard Newcombe.key_contributions: Established real-time high-density 3D reconstruction with KinectFusion, DTAM, and DynamicFusion. Project Aria.
Richard Newcombe.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in real-time high-density 3D reconstruction. Leading AR/VR perception with Meta Reality Labs.

Michael J. Black.topics: Human body 3D model (SMPL), optical flow, human pose/shape estimation, avatar
Michael J. Black.key_contributions: SMPL human body model (and MANO/FLAME/SMPL-X), robust optical flow, 3D reconstruction of human.
Michael J. Black.inclusion_reason: World authority on 3D human body modeling. SMPL is an industrial standard. Numerous test-of-time awards.

Antonio Torralba.topics: Scene recognition, large-scale image datasets (Places), network interpretability, multimodal learning
Antonio Torralba.key_contributions: Scene recognition, Places/Tiny Images dataset, Network Dissection, and related learning between vision and sound/language.
Antonio Torralba.inclusion_reason: Leading researcher in AI that can understand and interpret scenes. Winner of the Mark Everingham Prize and the Thomas Huang Prize.

Alexei A. Efros.topics: Texture synthesis, image conversion (pix2pix/CycleGAN), visual data mining, self-supervised learning
Alexei A. Efros.key_contributions: Texture synthesis (image quilting), pix2pix/CycleGAN infrastructure, data-driven vision, self-supervised learning.
Alexei A. Efros.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in image generation and compositing that bridges CV and CG. Awarded both SIGGRAPH and CV.

Jun-Yan Zhu.topics: Image generation/editing (GAN/Diffusion), image conversion (CycleGAN), generative model customization
Jun-Yan Zhu.key_contributions: CycleGAN, pix2pix (co-author), efficiency, customization, and data attribution of generative models.
Jun-Yan Zhu.inclusion_reason: A key researcher in image generation and conversion. First author of CycleGAN and leading generative model research.

Phillip Isola.topics: Image transformation (pix2pix), contrast learning (SimCLR system), representation learning, perceptual loss (LPIPS)
Phillip Isola.key_contributions: pix2pix image conversion, LPIPS perceptual distance, contrastive representation learning (CPC/SimCLR system).
Phillip Isola.inclusion_reason: Leading researcher in image transformation and representation learning. First author of pix2pix/LPIPS.

Andreas Geiger.topics: 3D scene understanding/reconstruction, autonomous driving (KITTI), neural implicit representation, generative 3D
Andreas Geiger.key_contributions: Neural 3D/generative scene representation such as KITTI benchmark, Occupancy Networks, GIRAFFE.
Andreas Geiger.inclusion_reason: Leading researcher in 3D scene understanding and autonomous driving. 10-year impact award at KITTI and CVPR Best Paper at GIRAFFE.

Vladlen Koltun.topics: 3D perception/reconstruction, simulation (CARLA), high-density prediction, depth estimation
Vladlen Koltun.key_contributions: CRF-RNN/dense CRF inference, CARLA simulator, robust monocular depth estimation (MiDaS/DPT), point cloud learning.
Vladlen Koltun.inclusion_reason: Leading researcher in 3D perception, simulation, and depth estimation. Apple's Distinguished Scientist.

Ming-Hsuan Yang.topics: Object tracking, video understanding/generation, image restoration, multimodal platform model
Ming-Hsuan Yang.key_contributions: A multimodal study of object tracking benchmarks, image/video restoration, generation and understanding.
Ming-Hsuan Yang.inclusion_reason: Leading researcher in object tracking and video understanding. Longuet-Higgins Award winner and top researcher from Taiwan.

Xiaoou Tang.topics: Face recognition (DeepID), deep learning, super resolution (SRCNN), image understanding
Xiaoou Tang.key_contributions: Founded DeepID facial recognition, SRCNN (deep super resolution), and MMLab. Co-founded SenseTime. Passed away in 2023.

Xiaogang Wang.topics: Face recognition, crowd analysis, person re-identification, object detection
Xiaogang Wang.key_contributions: Research on deep face recognition, crowd behavior analysis, person re-identification, and object detection. Co-founded SenseTime.

Kyoung Mu Lee.topics: Image restoration/super resolution, human pose estimation, optimization-based vision
Kyoung Mu Lee.key_contributions: Deep super resolution (EDSR etc.), 3D human pose estimation, optimization in vision. Editor-in-chief of TPAMI (first in Asia/Korea).

In So Kweon.topics: 3D vision, self-supervised learning, robotic perception, attention mechanism (CBAM)
In So Kweon.key_contributions: CBAM attention module, 3D reconstruction, and self-supervised depth estimation. Head of KAIST RCV Lab.

Baoquan Chen.topics: Point cloud learning (PointCNN), large-scale scene reconstruction, big data visualization, geometric processing
Baoquan Chen.key_contributions: PointCNN point cloud learning, large-scale city/scene reconstruction, and visualization. SIGGRAPH executive committee member.

Harry Heung-Yeung Shum.topics: Image-based rendering, image stitching/panorama, stereo, search engine technology

Yoichi Sato.topics: First-person (egocentric) video understanding, gaze estimation, human behavior sensing, object appearance modeling
Yoichi Sato.key_contributions: First-person video analysis, gaze estimation, human activity sensing. Leading the Japanese CV world.

Tatsuya Harada.topics: Image recognition, vision and language, medical imaging, intelligent robots
Tatsuya Harada.key_contributions: Image recognition/features, visual language, medical image analysis, robot learning.

Bolei Zhou.topics: CNN Interpretability (CAM), Scene Recognition (Places), Controllability of Generative Models, Embodied AI
Bolei Zhou.key_contributions: Class Activation Mapping (CAM), Places dataset, latent space control of GAN.

Song Han.topics: Model compression (Deep Compression), efficient inference, TinyML, efficient generative model
Song Han.key_contributions: Deep Compression (pruning/quantization), EIE (sparse matrix accelerator), TinyML, efficient image generation.
Song Han.inclusion_reason: Pioneer of efficient deep learning. A leading figure in model compression who also influenced NVIDIA Ampere sparse matrices.

Pat Hanrahan.topics: Physically based rendering (RenderMan), shading language, GPGPU, subsurface scattering
Pat Hanrahan.key_contributions: RenderMan interface/shading language, subsurface scattering, GPGPU (Brook).

Marc Levoy.topics: Volume rendering, light field, computational photography (Google Pixel), 3D scanning
Marc Levoy.key_contributions: Volume rendering, Light Field Rendering, Digital Michelangelo Project, Google Pixel computational photography.

Hugues Hoppe.topics: Surface reconstruction, progressive mesh, geometric image, texture synthesis
Hugues Hoppe.key_contributions: Surface reconstruction, progressive mesh, mesh optimization, geometric images, texture synthesis.

Demetri Terzopoulos.topics: Physics-based animation, deformable models (snakes), face/human body simulation, artificial life
Demetri Terzopoulos.key_contributions: Elastically deformable models (physics-based animation), active contours (snakes), face/human body simulation.

Ken Perlin.topics: Procedural noise (Perlin noise), texture synthesis, animation, VR/interaction
Ken Perlin.key_contributions: Perlin noise (fundamentals of procedural texture and natural phenomenon generation), animation, and futuristic VR.

Leonidas J. Guibas.topics: Computational geometry, 3D shape analysis, point cloud learning (PointNet system), geometric correspondence

Thomas Funkhouser.topics: 3D shape analysis/search, geometric modeling, scene understanding (ScanNet system), geometric processing

Niloy J. Mitra.topics: Shape analysis, symmetry/structure detection, structure-aware geometric processing, generative 3D modeling
Niloy J. Mitra.key_contributions: Shape symmetry/regularity detection, structure-aware geometric processing, modeling for fabrication.

Wenzel Jakob.topics: Physically based rendering, differentiable rendering, appearance modeling, Mitsuba renderer
Wenzel Jakob.key_contributions: OSS such as Mitsuba renderer, differentiable rendering (reverse rendering), appearance/material modeling, pybind11, etc.

Ravi Ramamoorthi.topics: Ambient light rendering (spherical harmonics), appearance acquisition, neural rendering (NeRF), light transport
Ravi Ramamoorthi.key_contributions: Co-author of the theory of illumination and reflection using spherical harmonics, appearance acquisition, and NeRF.

Ben Mildenhall.topics: Neural intensity field (NeRF), view synthesis, neural rendering

Jonathan T. Barron.topics: Neural brightness field (mip-NeRF), computational photography, inverse rendering, depth/shape estimation

Bernhard Kerbl.topics: 3D Gaussian Splatting, real-time rendering, GPU algorithm
Bernhard Kerbl.key_contributions: Lead author of 3D Gaussian Splatting. Real-time luminance field rendering and GPU implementation.

Yaser Sheikh.topics: Human body/face capture, volumetric avatar, pose estimation (OpenPose), social VR

Hao Li.topics: 3D avatar generation, facial animation, hair digitization, deepfake detection

Wojciech Matusik.topics: Computational fabrication/3D printing, material acquisition/BRDF, light field, differentiable design

Robin Rombach.topics: Latent diffusion model (Stable Diffusion), text image generation, generation base model

Robin Rombach.inclusion_reason: Lead author of Stable Diffusion (latent diffusion model). A central figure in the generative image model revolution.

Björn Ommer.topics: Latent diffusion model, generative model, visual composition/recognition, representation learning

Jiri Matas.topics: Local features (MSER), object tracking, robust estimation (RANSAC system), character recognition
Jiri Matas.key_contributions: MSER Local Features, Improving Robust Estimation, Object Tracking Benchmark (VOT).

Tinne Tuytelaars.topics: Local features (SURF), continuous learning, object recognition, visual language
Tinne Tuytelaars.key_contributions: SURF local features (co-authored), continuous learning (catastrophic forgetting), object recognition.

Tomas Pajdla.topics: Multi-view geometry, camera calibration, minimal solvers, 3D reconstruction
Tomas Pajdla.key_contributions: Omnidirectional/panoramic camera geometry, minimal solvers, Structure-from-Motion.

Dahua Lin.topics: Video understanding/action recognition, object detection, open source (OpenMMLab), large-scale model
Dahua Lin.key_contributions: OpenMMLab (MMDetection, etc.), video action recognition, object detection, large-scale learning platform.

Jonathan Ho.topics: Diffusion model (DDPM), text image generation (Imagen), video generation, classifier free guidance
Jonathan Ho.key_contributions: DDPM (Diffusion Probabilistic Model), Classifier-Free Guidance, Imagen, Video Diffusion.
Jonathan Ho.metrics: approx. 50,000+; rapidly increasing due to DDPM
Jonathan Ho.inclusion_reason: Lead author of DDPM, the basis of modern diffusion models. A central figure in the image/video generation revolution.

Ce Liu.topics: Optical flow, computational photography, scene analysis, self-supervised learning
Ce Liu.key_contributions: SIFT Flow/Dense correspondence, motion magnification (co-authored), computational photography, visual feature learning.

Toshihiko Yamasaki.topics: Multimedia analysis, image/video attractiveness estimation, pattern recognition, multimodal
Toshihiko Yamasaki.key_contributions: Multimedia content analysis, image/video attractiveness/popularity prediction, multimodal understanding.

Rakesh Agrawal.topics: Association rule mining, frequent patterns, privacy-preserving data mining, Apriori algorithm
Rakesh Agrawal.key_contributions: Proposed the Apriori algorithm and created the field of association rule mining. A pioneer in privacy-preserving data mining.

Jiawei Han.topics: Frequent pattern mining, FP-growth, heterogeneous information network, text mining
Jiawei Han.key_contributions: Proposed FP-growth algorithm. Author of the textbook "Data Mining: Concepts and Techniques". Pioneering heterogeneous information network mining.

Christos Faloutsos.topics: Graph mining, anomaly detection, time series indexing, power law
Christos Faloutsos.key_contributions: Established many foundations in large-scale graph mining, fractal dimension, and high-speed similarity search for time series (FastMap, etc.). Systematization of anomaly detection.

Philip S. Yu.topics: Stream mining, graph mining, clustering, social network analysis

Philip S. Yu.inclusion_reason: Highest number of citations. A giant of stream/network mining who was active in both industry (IBM) and academia.

Jian Pei.topics: Frequent pattern mining, sequence mining, PrefixSpan, skyline query
Jian Pei.key_contributions: Jointly developed core algorithms for frequent/sequence pattern mining such as FP-growth, PrefixSpan, and CLOSET.
Jian Pei.inclusion_reason: Co-proposer of the most important algorithms for pattern mining. Received SIGKDD Innovation Award.

Charu C. Aggarwal.topics: Anomaly detection, clustering, high-dimensional data, data stream
Charu C. Aggarwal.key_contributions: Basic research on anomaly detection, high-dimensional clustering, and data stream analysis. Author of many authoritative textbooks.
Charu C. Aggarwal.inclusion_reason: Systematization of anomaly detection/clustering and numerous standard textbooks. A researcher representing industry (IBM).

Hans-Peter Kriegel.topics: Clustering, DBSCAN, anomaly detection, spatial database, index
Hans-Peter Kriegel.key_contributions: Jointly developed core methods for density-based clustering and anomaly detection such as DBSCAN, OPTICS, and LOF (local outlier factor).
Hans-Peter Kriegel.inclusion_reason: The father of density-based clustering such as DBSCAN. A representative presence in European data mining.

Jure Leskovec.topics: Graph neural network, social network analysis, large-scale network, GraphSAGE
Jure Leskovec.key_contributions: Laid the foundations of network evolution models, GraphSAGE, node2vec, SNAP library, and PyG and led graph machine learning.
Jure Leskovec.inclusion_reason: A modern leader in graph mining and graph neural networks. Outstanding in terms of awards and influence.

George Karypis.topics: Graph partitioning, METIS, clustering, recommendation system, parallel data mining
George Karypis.key_contributions: Basics of METIS graph partitioning library, CHAMELEON clustering, recommender system and pattern mining.
George Karypis.inclusion_reason: An authority on graph partitioning and large-scale data mining. Latest SIGKDD Innovation Award Winner.

Huan Liu.topics: Feature selection, social media mining, dimensionality reduction, fake news detection
Huan Liu.key_contributions: Established the basic theory of feature selection and social media mining. Author of numerous standard surveys and textbooks.
Huan Liu.inclusion_reason: World authority on feature selection and social media mining. Received SIGKDD Innovation Award.

Bing Liu.topics: Sentiment analysis, opinion mining, web mining, continuous learning
Bing Liu.key_contributions: Established the field of sentiment analysis and opinion mining. A pioneer in association rule-based classification and web mining.
Bing Liu.inclusion_reason: Founder of sentiment analysis. Received the SIGKDD Innovation Award for bridging data mining and NLP.

Heikki Mannila.topics: Frequent episode mining, association rules, data mining theory, probabilistic method
Heikki Mannila.key_contributions: Frequent episode mining, theoretical foundations of data mining, co-author of the textbook "Principles of Data Mining".
Heikki Mannila.inclusion_reason: A European pioneer who laid the theoretical foundations of data mining. Received SIGKDD Innovation Award.

Pedro Domingos.topics: Markov logic network, statistical relationship learning, classification, scalable learning
Pedro Domingos.key_contributions: Proposal of Markov logic network, theoretical analysis of Naive Bayes, stream learning algorithms such as VFDT. Author of "The Master Algorithm".
Pedro Domingos.inclusion_reason: Pioneer of Markov logic networks and scalable learning. Received SIGKDD Innovation Award.

Vipin Kumar.topics: Parallel data mining, clustering, anomaly detection, earth science applications
Vipin Kumar.key_contributions: Co-authored the textbook "Introduction to Data Mining", parallel algorithms, network intrusion detection MINDS, climate data mining.

Jon Kleinberg.topics: HITS/Hub Authority, Small World Network, Social Network, Algorithmic Fairness
Jon Kleinberg.key_contributions: Many basic theories such as HITS algorithm and link analysis, small world navigation theory, burst detection, etc.

Thorsten Joachims.topics: SVM(SVMlight), learning ranking, learning from click data, counterfactual learning
Thorsten Joachims.key_contributions: SVMlight, SVM application to text classification, learning ranking from click data, basics of counterfactual learning.
Thorsten Joachims.inclusion_reason: A pioneer in the use of learning rankings and click logs. Received SIGKDD Innovation Award.

Johannes Gehrke.topics: data streams, differential privacy, distributed systems, decision trees
Johannes Gehrke.key_contributions: Practical implementation of scalable decision trees (RainForest/BOAT), data stream processing, and differential privacy. Co-author of the textbook "Database Management Systems".
Johannes Gehrke.inclusion_reason: Contributed in both database and data mining fields. A researcher from both industry and academia who received the SIGKDD Innovation Award.

Raghu Ramakrishnan.topics: Query optimization, deductive database, cloud data system, data mining
Raghu Ramakrishnan.key_contributions: Textbook "Database Management Systems", deductive DB CORAL, CURE/BIRCH clustering, cloud data system at Yahoo!/Microsoft.

Michael Stonebraker.topics: Relational database system, column-oriented DB, stream processing, NewSQL, scientific database
Michael Stonebraker.key_contributions: Constructed innovative DB systems one after another, including Ingres, Postgres, C-Store/Vertica, H-Store/VoltDB, and Aurora/StreamBase.
Michael Stonebraker.inclusion_reason: The only Turing Award winner in the database field. The first Codd Innovations Award. A giant of classic and real systems.

Jim Gray.topics: Transaction processing, ACID, concurrency control, disaster recovery, scientific data management
Jim Gray.key_contributions: Established transaction concepts, ACID characteristics, locking and recovery theory, TPC benchmarks, SkyServer, etc.
Jim Gray.inclusion_reason: Turing Award winner who established transaction processing theory. The most important classic figure in database history.

David DeWitt.topics: Parallel database, query execution, benchmarking, big data processing
David DeWitt.key_contributions: Gamma parallel database, Wisconsin benchmark, fundamentals of parallel query processing, and contributions to the MapReduce debate.
David DeWitt.inclusion_reason: Father of parallel database systems. A classic giant that won the Codd Innovations Award.

Hector Garcia-Molina.topics: Distributed database, data integration, information retrieval, digital library, P2P
Hector Garcia-Molina.key_contributions: Distributed Transactions, TSIMMIS Data Integration, Stanford WebBase/Digital Library, Textbook "Database Systems: The Complete Book".
Hector Garcia-Molina.inclusion_reason: A giant in data integration and distributed DB. A legendary Stanford researcher who trained the co-founders of Google.

Jeffrey D. Ullman.topics: Database theory, deductive database, MapReduce algorithm, large-scale data mining
Jeffrey D. Ullman.key_contributions: Basics of database theory and deductive DB, many textbooks (compilers, algorithms, DB, "Mining of Massive Datasets").
Jeffrey D. Ullman.inclusion_reason: Turing Award Winner. Transgenerational impact on database theory and large-scale data mining textbooks.

Jennifer Widom.topics: Data stream (STREAM), active database, uncertain data, data provenance
Jennifer Widom.key_contributions: STREAM data stream management system, Trio uncertain database, active DB, textbook "A First Course in Database Systems".
Jennifer Widom.inclusion_reason: Pioneer in stream processing and uncertain data. Representative female researcher who received the Codd Innovations Award.

Surajit Chaudhuri.topics: Query optimization, automatic tuning, data cleansing, approximate query processing
Surajit Chaudhuri.key_contributions: Basic research on self-tuning database (AutoAdmin), query optimization, data cleansing, and approximate query processing.
Surajit Chaudhuri.inclusion_reason: An authority on query optimization and automatic tuning. Codd Award winner representing industry (Microsoft).

Gerhard Weikum.topics: Knowledge base (YAGO), information extraction, transactions, semantic search
Gerhard Weikum.key_contributions: YAGO knowledge base, large-scale information extraction, transaction management, textbook "Transactional Information Systems".
Gerhard Weikum.inclusion_reason: European giant of knowledge base and information extraction. Influence the world with YAGO. Received the Codd Innovations Award.

Anastasia Ailamaki.topics: Database architecture, cache-aware processing, scientific data management, memory hierarchy optimization
Anastasia Ailamaki.key_contributions: Research on hardware-aware database architecture, DBMS cache performance analysis, and scientific data management systems.
Anastasia Ailamaki.inclusion_reason: A leading expert on hardware-aware data systems. Representative female researcher who received the Codd Innovations Award.

Samuel Madden.topics: Column-oriented DB (C-Store), sensor network (TinyDB), stream processing, mobile data management
Samuel Madden.key_contributions: TinyDB/TAG sensor network queries, C-Store/Vertica columnar DB, CarTel, and recently data system and ML integration.
Samuel Madden.inclusion_reason: Pioneer of column-oriented DB and sensor DB. Active leader of the latest Codd Innovations Award winners.

Joseph M. Hellerstein.topics: Scalable query processing, interactive data analysis, declarative networking, cloud data systems

Joseph M. Hellerstein.inclusion_reason: An authority on extensible query processing and interactive data analysis. Codd Innovations Award winning active leader.

Dan Suciu.topics: Probabilistic database, query complexity, semi-structured data/XML, data provenance
Dan Suciu.key_contributions: Theoretical foundations of probabilistic databases, dichotomy theorem for query evaluation, semi-structured data, and theory of data provenance.
Dan Suciu.inclusion_reason: A leading expert on probabilistic databases and query complexity theory. Received the Codd Innovations Award.

Alon Halevy.topics: Data integration, schema mapping, web tables, data lake
Alon Halevy.key_contributions: Theory of data integration (LAV/GAV, answered queries), Google Fusion Tables/WebTables, data lakes and data discovery.

Beng Chin Ooi.topics: Large-scale data management system, blockchain DB, distributed learning system, high-dimensional index
Beng Chin Ooi.key_contributions: epiC/BestPeer P2P data system, SINGA deep learning system, BlockBench blockchain DB evaluation, high-dimensional index.
Beng Chin Ooi.inclusion_reason: A data system researcher representing Asia (Singapore). Codd Innovations Award Winner: Diversity Matters.

Masaru Kitsuregawa.topics: Parallel/high performance database, hash join, big data infrastructure, information explosion
Masaru Kitsuregawa.key_contributions: GRACE hash join algorithm, parallel/high performance database architecture, and construction of big data research infrastructure.

Serge Abiteboul.topics: Database theory, semi-structured data/XML, web data management, distributed knowledge base
Serge Abiteboul.key_contributions: Textbook "Foundations of Databases", semi-structured data and XML theory, ActiveXML, web data management.
Serge Abiteboul.inclusion_reason: Author of the standard textbook on database theory. Received the Codd Award as a representative of European database theory.

Ronald Fagin.topics: Database theory, normal form (4NF), top-k query (TA algorithm), finite model theory, schema mapping
Ronald Fagin.key_contributions: Fourth normal form, Fagin's theorem (finite model theory), threshold algorithm for top-k search, data exchange theory.

Goetz Graefe.topics: Query optimization, Volcano/Cascades optimizer, parallel query execution, B-trees and sorting
Goetz Graefe.key_contributions: Research on Volcano/Cascades query optimizer (Basics of SQL Server), parallel query execution model, B-trees and sorting algorithms.

Laura Haas.topics: Data integration (Garlic/Clio), distributed query optimization, information integration, data curation
Laura Haas.key_contributions: Fundamentals of Starburst extensible DBMS, Garlic heterogeneous integration, Clio schema mapping, and IBM information integration products.
Laura Haas.inclusion_reason: A female researcher who is a pioneer in data integration and represents industry (IBM). Received the Codd Innovations Award.

C. Mohan.topics: Transaction processing, ARIES recovery, concurrency control, blockchain
C. Mohan.key_contributions: ARIES recovery/concurrency control algorithm (base of major DB), distributed transaction commit protocol.

Moshe Y. Vardi.topics: Query complexity, finite model theory, dependency theory, model checking
Moshe Y. Vardi.key_contributions: Computational complexity of query evaluation (data complexity/join complexity), finite model theory, DB theory using logic and automata.
Moshe Y. Vardi.inclusion_reason: A giant in database theory and computational theory. This is an exceptional achievement, having won both the Gödel Award and the Codd Award.

Martin Kersten.topics: Column-oriented DB (MonetDB), main memory DB, database architecture, scientific data management
Martin Kersten.key_contributions: Development of MonetDB columnar database, main memory DB architecture, self-organizing index including database cracking.
